commit.message:
fix: login deadlock — Singleton provider with preferences fallback

ClaimsActiveUserProvider caused deadlocks in Blazor Server:
1. Scoped registration couldn't be resolved from Singleton repos (null)
2. AuthenticationStateProvider.GetAwaiter().GetResult() deadlocked the
   sync context

New approach: Singleton provider that uses HttpContext.User when
available (HTTP requests, prerender) and falls back to preferences
for Blazor SignalR calls. The login endpoint already writes
active_profile_id to preferences, so this value is current.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Feature/markdown packages (#7)

* feat: add SHA-256 hash verification and Windows Sandbox testing

  Introduce installer hash checking (verify command) and an end-to-end
  sandbox testing workflow (sandbox command) for generated PSADT packages.

  - Add optional sha256 field to Installer schema with hex validation
  - Add packager/hash.py for compute/verify with HashMismatchError
  - Capture InstallerSha256 from winget manifests in winget-scaffold
  - Add verify CLI command and auto-check in generate
  - Add packager/sandbox.py for .wsb config generation
  - Add sandbox_test.ps1.j2 and test_detection.ps1.j2 templates
  - Add sandbox CLI command with full install/detect/uninstall cycle
  - Update README.md and CLAUDE.md with new commands and fields

* Add markdown-driven package prototype for AI generation

Demonstrates simplified approach where:
- Markdown files with YAML frontmatter define packages
- AI/generator reads instructions and produces PSADT v4 PowerShell
- 150 lines vs 500+ in current CLI
- Human-readable docs that double as agent instructions

Includes:
- packages/firefox.package.md - example definition
- prototype/generate.py - minimal generator
- Generated Invoke-AppDeployToolkit.ps1 sample

* Complete Markdown-driven package system with LLM support

Major additions:
- llm_generator.py: LLM integration (Claude/OpenAI) + rule-based fallback
- PowerShellValidator: Ensures PSADT v4 compliance (Open-ADTSession, Start-ADTProcess, etc.)
- winget_scaffold.py: Auto-generate .package.md from winget-pkgs
- Updated generate.py: Clean CLI with --validate-only option

New example packages:
- firefox.package.md: EXE with registry detection
- 7zip.package.md: MSI with product code
- 7-zip.package.md: Auto-generated from winget
- vscode.package.md: Complex with post-install actions

Features:
- ~200 lines vs 500+ in original CLI
- Human-readable Markdown doubles as documentation
- Automatic PSADT v4 validation
- Works with or without LLM API key
- Winget integration for auto-population

To use with AI:
   = '...'
  uv run python -m prototype.generate packages\app.package.md

* Add .env file support for API keys

- Added python-dotenv dependency
- llm_generator.py now loads .env automatically
- Created .env.example as template
- Added .env to .gitignore (API keys should not be committed)
- Updated README with .env instructions

Usage:
  1. copy .env.example .env
  2. Edit .env with your ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
  3. uv run python -m prototype.generate packages\app.package.md

* Update README and add comprehensive CLAUDE.md

README.md:
- Restructured to highlight new Markdown-driven approach as primary
- Added quick start guide with .env setup
- Documented both Markdown and legacy YAML workflows
- Updated project layout to show both systems
- Added comparison table showing before/after

.claude/CLAUDE.md:
- Comprehensive agent instructions for Claude Code sessions
- Clear guidance on when to use Markdown vs YAML approach
- PSADT v4 command reference (MUST vs MUST NOT)
- Package definition format with examples
- Essential commands for daily work
- Validation rules explained
- Common tasks and debugging guidance
- Code style and git workflow

Both files now provide thorough context for AI agents working in this repo.

* Add configurable LLM model selection

- ANTHROPIC_MODEL and OPENAI_MODEL env vars

- Defaults: claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022, gpt-4

- Updated .env.example and all docs

* Clean up unnecessary files and directories

Changes:

- Updated .gitignore to exclude out/, sandbox/, .tmp/, scratch-verify/, .ruff_cache/

- Removed duplicate CLAUDE.md from root (keeping .claude/CLAUDE.md)

- Deleted demo.ps1

- Removed generated directories (out/, sandbox/, scratch-verify/)

- Removed legacy profiles/ directory (migrated to packages/)

* Add configurable API base URLs for OpenAI and Anthropic

- Added ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL and OPENAI_BASE_URL environment variables

- Updated LLMGenerator to accept and use custom base URLs

- Supports Azure OpenAI, corporate proxies, local LLMs (Ollama, etc.)

- Updated .env.example with base URL options and documentation

- Updated all documentation (README.md, CLAUDE.md, prototype/README.md)

- Maintains backward compatibility (defaults to official API endpoints)

* Fix .env file comment formatting and add testing guide

- Fixed bullet points in .env.example (lines 45-48) to use proper comment syntax

- Fixed same issue in existing .env file

- Added TESTING.md with comprehensive testing workflow

- All tests pass: rule-based generation, winget scaffold, validation

* Fix provider auto-detection in generate_with_fallback

- LLMGenerator now auto-detects provider (claude/openai) based on available API keys

- generate_with_fallback no longer hardcodes provider='claude'

- Added logging to show which provider and model is being used

- Falls back to rule-based if AI generation fails or has validation issues

* Strengthen LLM system prompt for better PSADT v4 compliance

- Added explicit NEVER USE list with actual strings to avoid

- Added REQUIRED Script Structure section with exact order

- Included example syntax for critical functions

- Changed timeout from 60s to 120s for slower endpoints

- Fixed all SyntaxWarnings in prompt

- Improved validation: now logs which provider/model is being used

- Added LLM output logging when validation fails for debugging

* Rename project references from Payload to packager

Updated documentation and code comments:

- README.md: Title, badges, clone instructions

- TESTING.md: Title and description

- .claude/CLAUDE.md: Title and description

- packager/winget.py: Comments and User-Agent string

GitHub repo renamed from Payload to packager, local remote URL updated

* Update PR template for new Markdown-driven workflow

- Updated testing checklist to include Markdown generation commands

* Add comprehensive test suite for new prototype system

- test_markdown.py: 11 tests for PackageDefinition parsing

- test_llm_generator.py: 22 tests for LLM and validation

- test_prototype_cli.py: 16 tests for generate CLI commands

- Fixed LLMGenerator to accept api_key parameter

- Red/green TDD ready for future changes

- Prevents tests from attempting real LLM API calls (which time out)

- All 51 new tests now complete in ~3 seconds (was timing out at 60+ seconds)

* feat: auto-download installers and PSADT, harden PSADT v4 generation

* feat: harden PSADT v4 generation against weak models and scaffold bugs

Triggered by a Slack package that installed correctly but failed to

packager/winget.py:

* fix: splicer must require language tag; scaffolder TODO now throws

Root cause of a silent uninstall failure reported against the VSCodium
package: the body-fence splicer was too permissive and inlined a plain

PowerShell. The generated uninstall block contained the literal
command `uv run python -m packager.probe "VSCodium" --emit-package-md`,

* feat: cache research findings and short-circuit winget-known packages

Before: every `generate` run made 3 Tavily API calls per package,
regardless of whether winget already had authoritative silent args,
and re-scaffolding the same package re-paid the full cost every time.
A batch of 20 packages burned 60 credits; most of them unnecessary.

After:
- Winget-known packages (Firefox, 7zip, VSCode, VSCodium, Slack, etc.)
  trigger a short-circuit in build_deployment_plan: if winget already
  provided silent args, or the installer is a plain MSI, search_vendor_docs
  is not called at all. The plan still gets a `tavily-skipped` marker
  finding so the LLM prompt shows the reasoning.
- When Tavily IS needed, search_vendor_docs is now ONE query (down from
  three): `<installer_filename> command line silent install uninstall`.
  Using the installer filename gives us version+vendor specificity for
  free, and naturally busts the cache on a version bump.
- Every Tavily call is wrapped by an on-disk JSON cache keyed on
  sha256(app_name|version|query). Repeated generations for the same
  (app, version) hit the cache and cost zero credits for 30 days.

packager/cache.py (new):
- ResearchCache with get/set/clear. Atomic writes via json.dumps.
- Cross-platform cache directory:
    * Windows: %LOCALAPPDATA%\packager\research-cache\
    * Linux/macOS: $XDG_CACHE_HOME/packager/research/ or ~/.cache/packager/research/
- TTL default 30 days, override via PACKAGER_RESEARCH_CACHE_TTL_DAYS.
- Cache location override via PACKAGER_RESEARCH_CACHE_DIR.
- Disable entirely with PACKAGER_NO_RESEARCH_CACHE=1.
- Every I/O path degrades to a silent no-op on failure so the cache
  never breaks generation.

packager/research.py:
- New _build_research_query() helper returns the single specific query.
- search_vendor_docs() looks up the cache first, writes back on miss.
- build_deployment_plan() skips search_vendor_docs() when winget has
  silent args or installer_kind == "msi"; override with
  PACKAGER_FORCE_WEB_RESEARCH=1.

tests/test_cache.py (new, 14 tests):
- Round-trip, miss-on-different-{app,version,query}, clear, clear on
  missing dir, TTL expiry, TTL env override (valid + invalid), disabled
  via env (get and set), cache dir env override, default dir shape.

tests/test_research.py (+5 tests):
- Cache hit on second search_vendor_docs call with Tavily mocked to
  count calls.
- Short-circuit fires when winget has silent args.
- Short-circuit does NOT fire without winget evidence.
- PACKAGER_FORCE_WEB_RESEARCH=1 bypasses the short-circuit.
- Existing parse-Tavily-json test now pins a tmp cache dir so it's
  hermetic.

Expected credit impact for a batch of 20 packages where 15 are
winget-known and 5 are custom:
  before: 60 Tavily calls
  after:   5 Tavily calls on the first run, 0 on subsequent runs
           (92% reduction, trending to 100% with warm cache)

Tests: 170 passing (+18: 14 cache + 5 research + 1 reshaped).

perf: isinstance fast path, lock-free StoreAttr, safe try elimination, global Arc dedup

  itself. Eliminates execute_one dispatch. (+133%, 3.9M → 9.1M ops/s)

  the same global value (Arc::ptr_eq check). (+34%, 14.7M → 19.7M ops/s)

  (LOAD_CONST, STORE_FAST, etc. that cannot raise). (+17%, 11.1M → 13.0M)

commit.message:
fix: add missing scripts to git, fix Windows .cmd shim CRLF, add cross-platform e2e tests (#19)

- Add scripts/build.js and scripts/postinstall.js to git tracking (they were
  present locally but never committed, causing GitHub installs to fail entirely)
- Fix scripts/postinstall.js: .join(chr(92)r chr(92)n) produced literal backslash-r-backslash-n
  text instead of CRLF; the .cmd shim was one malformed line that never ran node
- Fix scripts/prepare.js: was running bare tsc (unbundled output) instead of the
  esbuild build; unbundled output can't find react/ink at runtime in global installs
- Add prepare script to package.json so npm runs the esbuild build automatically
  on
pm install -g github:... installs
- Add .github/workflows/e2e.yml: cross-platform e2e tests (ubuntu/macos/windows)
  that validate plugin files, piped-mode rendering, and the global ccfooter-config
  command (including the Windows .cmd shim specifically)

commit.message:
fix: Singleton→Scoped DI resolution for IActiveUserProvider

Repositories are Singleton but IActiveUserProvider is Scoped on the server.
Changed from constructor caching to per-call lazy resolution via
_serviceProvider.GetService<IActiveUserProvider>() to avoid
Singleton-consumes-Scoped InvalidOperationException at runtime.

Applied to all 5 repositories + ProgressCacheService.
